definition:
δικαστής, -οῦ, ὁ
 (δικάζω), [in LXX for שָׁפַט ;] 
a judge: Act.7:27, 35" (LXX) .†
 SYN.: κριτής, WH. "gives prominence to the mental process" (Thayer). δ. is the forensic term. In Attic law, the δικασταί were jurors, with a κριτής as presiding officer (LS, see word; Enc. Brit. 11, xii, 504 f.)  (AS)
